target.title: fix(telegram): preserve forum topic routing
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep Telegram thread-not-found fallback for General topic (`message_thread_id=1`), while failing closed for explicit non-General forum topic sends and direct/private topic sends.
- Preserve Telegram topic routing for automatic async/heartbeat follow-ups by reusing stored topic thread IDs for plugin-owned group routes.
- Seed explicit cron announce targets into isolated-session delivery context so completion and heartbeat notices stay on the configured topic instead of falling back to an agent default group.
- Keep the effective diff focused on Telegram topic routing, cron delivery context, outbound target reconstruction, and the temporary current-main memory-core test-contract shim needed for CI.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Tests only

## Scope

Telegram extension outbound delivery, shared heartbeat target resolution, isolated cron session delivery metadata, and one test-only `memory-core` mock compatibility update for the current subagent runtime contract.

## Linked Issue / PR

- #78755 independently reported missing `message_thread_id` on outbound Telegram forum sends and was closed as duplicate/superseded by the same topic-routing work.
- #78724 covered the final session auto-reply `message_thread_id` pass-through now present on `main`; this PR keeps the broader fail-closed and async-route preservation behavior.
- Adjacent historical thread/topic work reviewed: #43808, #49704, #52824, #52286, #73900, #12293, #30892.

## Root Cause

The Telegram sender retried `400: message thread not found` by removing `message_thread_id` too broadly. That is safe only for General-topic recovery, but unsafe for explicit non-General forum topics and direct/private topic sends because Telegram can then deliver into the wrong root context.

A separate async path could reconstruct heartbeat/completion delivery from incomplete session metadata. Topic-scoped Telegram sessions and explicit cron announce jobs could lose the thread id before later automatic notices were sent.

## Regression Test Plan

- Explicit forum topic text/media/sticker/poll sends reject after the first thread-not-found error and do not retry threadless.
- Topic-qualified recipient strings such as `telegram:group:<id>:topic:<thread>` fail closed.
- Direct/private and resolved username DM sends with explicit thread ids reject after the first thread-not-found error and do not retry threadless.
- Draft stream preview/materialize sends fail closed for non-General forum topics and keep the existing stricter DM-preview behavior.
- Heartbeat target resolution preserves stored thread IDs for plugin-owned group routes when only `deliveryContext` or `origin.chatType` carries the topic context.
- Explicit Telegram cron announce targets persist session delivery context with the resolved chat id and topic thread id.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram topic sends must not silently retry without `message_thread_id` after Telegram reports `message thread not found`; async heartbeat/completion delivery must preserve the intended topic route instead of falling back to a group/default route.
- Real environment tested: Disposable OpenClaw staging gateway on NixOS Linux, PR worktree `/tmp/openclaw-pr76920-merge`, head `f515e5d36ac1166b955838c21489390dbfd9dbe2`, using `@kesslerClawBot` in a real Telegram forum group (`-1003908474243`). The staging gateway was started from this PR head, and the proof script used the PR's built Telegram runtime, `sendMessageTelegram` from `dist/extensions/telegram/runtime-api.js`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the disposable gateway with `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/home/art/.local/state/openclaw-staging/disposable/openclaw.json` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/home/art/.local/state/openclaw-staging/disposable/state`, then ran a redacted proof script from `/tmp/openclaw-pr76920-merge` that exercised: root chat-scoped send to `-1003908474243`, explicit General topic `-1003908474243:topic:1`, invalid explicit non-General topic `-1003908474243:topic:999999`, and valid explicit non-General topic `-1003908474243:topic:16`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied live output from the staging gateway/proof run. Full PR comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/76920#issuecomment-4559064171

  Key copied output:

  ```text
  head=f515e5d36ac1166b955838c21489390dbfd9dbe2
  chat=-1003908474243
  generalTopic=1
  [telegram] outbound send ok accountId=default chatId=-1003908474243 messageId=14 operation=sendMessage deliveryKind=text chunkCount=1
  rootSend=ok messageId=14 chatId=-1003908474243
  [telegram] outbound send ok accountId=default chatId=-1003908474243 messageId=15 operation=sendMessage deliveryKind=text chunkCount=1
  generalTopicSend=ok messageId=15 chatId=-1003908474243 topic=1
  [telegram] message failed: Call to 'sendMessage' failed! (400: Bad Request: message thread not found)
  invalidTopicSend=failed_closed topic=999999
  invalidTopicCheck=no_threadless_retry_observed

  nonGeneralTopicProof=head=f515e5d36ac1166b955838c21489390dbfd9dbe2
  nonGeneralTopic=16
  [telegram] outbound send ok accountId=default chatId=-1003908474243 messageId=17 operation=sendMessage deliveryKind=text threadId=16 chunkCount=1
  nonGeneralTopicSend=ok messageId=17 chatId=-1003908474243 topic=16
  ```
- Observed result after fix: Root chat-scoped send succeeded without a topic/thread id; General topic delivery succeeded and remained chat-scoped; an invalid explicit non-General forum topic failed closed and did not retry threadless; a valid explicit non-General forum topic sent with `threadId=16`, proving delivery stayed in the requested forum topic.
- What was not tested: I did not run a full live cron or heartbeat follow-up through the gateway in this proof pass. Existing focused tests still cover cron session delivery context and heartbeat topic-route reconstruction.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The live proof covers the real Telegram transport and topic send path. Cron/heartbeat route reconstruction remains covered by focused regression tests rather than a scheduled end-to-end cron/heartbeat run.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): ClawSweeper's current-main/source review for this PR identifies the pre-patch failure mode: explicit Telegram topic sends could retry threadless after `message thread not found`, and async follow-up routing could lose topic context. The after-fix proof above shows the explicit send path no longer retries threadless for an invalid non-General topic.

## Verification

Current head `75acdc52684b07c8355966394f5ad5347a713e0a` was refreshed against latest `origin/main`, trimmed back to the intended nine-file effective diff, and pushed after the commands above passed locally.

## Security Impact

Reduces cross-topic message leakage in Telegram forum groups and private/direct topic contexts by failing closed when an explicit thread is stale or invalid, and by preserving topic context for automatic follow-up notices.

## Compatibility / Migration

No config migration. Non-General forum topic and direct/private topic sends now surface the Telegram error instead of silently retrying threadless. Explicit cron announce routes now persist the intended delivery context for later automatic notices.

## Risks

If an operator intentionally relied on stale explicit topic sends falling back to a root chat, those sends now fail. That behavior was the leakage bug this PR is meant to prevent.

The cron/session change is intentionally limited to resolved explicit delivery targets; bare `delivery.mode: none` jobs still avoid implicit last-target resolution.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted refresh, simplification, validation, and PR-body update.

target.title: feat(plugins): add system-tier plugin discovery for machine-wide policy  enforcement"
target.body:
## Summary

  - Adds a new `"system"` plugin origin loaded from a machine-wide directory (`/etc/openclaw/plugins` on Linux/Mac,
  `%ProgramData%\OpenClaw\plugins` on Windows)
  - System plugins are immune to user config (deny/allow lists, per-entry disable, global toggle)
  - System plugins get implicit `allowConversationAccess` and `allowPromptInjection` permissions
  - System plugin hooks run at priority >= 10000 (before all user/bundled hooks)

  This enables SYSTEM services to deploy policy-enforcement plugins that intercept the full agent loop (tool calls,
  prompts, model selection, outbound messages) without requiring user registration or CLI interaction.

  ## Changed files

  | File | Change |
  |---|---|
  | `plugin-origin.types.ts` | Added `"system"` to `PluginOrigin` union |
  | `roots.ts` | `resolveSystemPluginsDir()` with platform-aware defaults + test override setter |
  | `discovery.ts` | System tier scanned before all other sources |
  | `config-activation-shared.ts` | System origin bypasses all activation checks |
  | `registry.ts` | Implicit permissions + priority floor for system hooks |
  | `loader.ts` | System dir in cache key |
  | `source-display.ts` | Format system plugin sources in CLI output |

  ## Test plan

  - [x] `roots.test.ts` — 5 tests: platform resolution (Windows, Linux, macOS)
  - [x] `system-plugin-activation.test.ts` — 8 tests: bypass deny/allow/disable, non-system still blocked
  - [x] `system-plugin-discovery.test.ts` — 5 tests: discovery with origin=system, precedence, empty/missing dir
  - [x] `system-plugin-loader.test.ts` — 7 tests: full loader integration, permissions, priority ordering
  - [x] `system-plugin-blocking.test.ts` — 9 tests: tool call blocking, message cancellation, agent reply short-circuit,
   transcript suppression, terminal semantics
  - [x] Existing `loader.test.ts` — 122/122 pass (no regressions)
  - [x] Existing `discovery.test.ts` — 48 pass (no regressions)
  - [x] Existing `source-display.test.ts` — 4/4 pass (no regressions)
  - [x] Plugin contract tests — 734/734 pass
  - [x] Import boundary check — clean

  - [x] AI-assisted: built with Claude Code
  - [x] Degree of testing: **fully tested** (38 new tests + all existing pass)
  - [x] Confirm understanding of code changes

  🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: feat(cli): add openclaw experimental for toggling experimental config flags
target.body:
> AI-assisted. Tested on pushed branch `115daf62fe`: focused Vitest coverage for config/CLI/chat/TUI passed, config schema check passed, the real `pnpm openclaw experimental --help` entrypoint resolved, the interactive CLI picker wrote a throwaway config with visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-2YeaK01TZ6WTsES0OdmQ9o.gif), a real TUI selector flow has visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif), and a rebuilt Docker gateway exercised `/experimental` over WebSocket with persisted config writes.

## Summary

- **Problem:** OpenClaw has experimental config flags spread through the config schema, but the user-facing toggle surface was drifting toward a CLI-only implementation. That would make `/experimental` unavailable from chat/TUI/channel command surfaces and would duplicate the flag registry outside the config contract.
- **Why it matters:** Experimental flags affect runtime behavior and should use one bounded, auditable config subset across CLI, chat, TUI, and channel plugins instead of hard-coded per-surface lists.
- **What changed:** Added a shared experimental-flag helper that discovers boolean config leaves under `experimental` from the base config schema and schema UI hints. The CLI `openclaw experimental`, chat `/experimental`, TUI command handling, and channel-command registry now use that same subset.
- **Config command policy:** Added `commands.experimental` so `/experimental` can be enabled or disabled alongside other configurable command surfaces.
- **What did not change:** No new experimental flags were introduced. No secrets, network calls, or provider runtime behavior changed.

## Scope Note

This PR started as a small CLI picker, but review feedback exposed a broader product/security boundary: if experimental config can be edited from one surface, the implementation should not create a CLI-only registry or drift from chat, TUI, and channel command handling. The current shape is intentionally larger because it moves `/experimental` behind a shared schema-derived subset and a configurable command gate instead of adding another one-off config editor.

The scope is still kept to the minimum shared surface needed for that boundary: no new experimental flags, no arbitrary config editor, no new network/runtime capability, no SDK export, and no separate per-surface experimental registry.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CLI
- [x] Config
- [x] Messaging / channel command surface
- [x] TUI
- [x] Docs

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: Discord suggestion from Json in #general
- Reviewer concern addressed: Peter Steinberger asked that this avoid config-schema drift, consider `/experimental` in chat/TUI, and keep config editing security-bounded.
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A - new feature and follow-up design correction.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

Focused local tests cover the shared flag registry, CLI apply behavior, chat command parsing/gating, channel command listing, and TUI command handling.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New top-level command: `openclaw experimental`
- New slash command: `/experimental`
- TUI command handling can list and update experimental flags through the same shared registry
- `commands.experimental` controls whether the experimental chat/channel command is enabled
- Experimental flags are discovered from the config schema instead of duplicated in each surface

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Config schema + UI hints
        |
        v
shared experimental flag subset
        |
        +-- openclaw experimental
        +-- /experimental in chat/channel command handling
        +-- TUI command backend/handlers
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No. The command writes only existing config paths that users can already edit through config tooling.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes, `/experimental` is a new configurable command surface. It is gated by `commands.experimental` and is limited to schema-discovered boolean config leaves whose path contains an `experimental` segment.
- Data access scope changed? No.
- Security boundary: this does not make arbitrary config editable from chat/TUI. The editable subset is derived from the config schema and narrowed to experimental boolean leaves only.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu source checkout in Codex worktree
- Runtime: Node 22 / pnpm
- Branch SHA: `115daf62fe`
- Docker proof image: `openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest` (image `sha256:00f28c0f1a87487dd060b0847f6943b219dd50ccb7e82c7936225c0f47715526`)
- Docker proof gateway: `pr76298-exp-proof-gateway`, `127.0.0.1:19982->18789`, health `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`

### Commands run locally

```text
OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=180000 node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/config/experimental-flags.test.ts src/config/experimental-config-file.test.ts src/commands/experimental.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-gating.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-parse.test.ts src/tui/commands.test.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts
git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
pnpm config:schema:check
pnpm openclaw experimental --help
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr76298-cli-picker-proof.json pnpm openclaw experimental
jq . /tmp/openclaw-pr76298-cli-picker-proof.json
OPENCLAW_IMAGE=openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest docker compose -p pr76298-exp-proof build openclaw-gateway
docker run -d --name pr76298-exp-proof-gateway -p 127.0.0.1:19982:18789 ... openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest node dist/index.js gateway --bind lan --port 18789
curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:19982/healthz
docker exec pr76298-exp-proof-gateway node --input-type=module -e '<WebSocket /experimental proof script>'
jq . /tmp/openclaw-pr76298-docker-proof/config/openclaw.json
```

### Expected

The schema-derived experimental flag subset is stable, CLI/chat/TUI surfaces use it consistently, `/experimental` obeys command gating, the real CLI entrypoint resolves the new command, and a gateway client with the right operator scopes can list and toggle the bounded experimental config subset.

### Actual

Verified on pushed branch `115daf62fe`.

## Evidence

- [x] Focused Vitest run passed 5 shards / 128 tests after the final type-only commit.
- [x] `git diff --check` passed before the final commit; `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed for the final type-only commit.
- [x] `pnpm config:schema:check` passed (`[base-config-schema] ok`).
- [x] `pnpm openclaw experimental --help` executed through the real repo CLI entrypoint at SHA `c354c59`.
- [x] Interactive CLI picker executed on head `115daf6`, selected `agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, printed the updated-config summary, and persisted the expected throwaway config. Slow VHS GIF: https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-2YeaK01TZ6WTsES0OdmQ9o.gif
- [x] TUI selector proof executed through the real `runTui` loop with an isolated fixture backend. The TUI opened `/experimental`, listed experimental flags, selected `agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, printed `experimental enabled: agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, and logged `setExperimentalFlag`. VHS GIF: https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif
- [x] Docker image rebuilt after the final commit and the proof container ran from image `sha256:00f28c0f1a87487dd060b0847f6943b219dd50ccb7e82c7936225c0f47715526`.
- [x] Dockered gateway health returned `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`.
- [x] WebSocket `chat.send` proof through the gateway returned real `Config updated` replies for `/experimental off tools.experimental.planTool` and `/experimental on agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`.
- [x] Persisted config contains `tools.experimental.planTool: false`, `agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean: true`, and `meta.lastTouchedVersion: "2026.5.22"`.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: schema-derived flag listing/apply helpers, CLI command behavior, chat parse/gating/list/set behavior, TUI command handling, embedded trusted-local TUI config path, live CLI registration/help output, interactive CLI picker write behavior, real TUI selector behavior with an isolated backend, and Dockered gateway command behavior over WebSocket.
- Edge cases checked: disabled command gating, unknown flag selectors, no-op flag updates, invalid snapshots, validation failures, absent unchecked picker flags becoming explicit `false`, and boolean-only experimental subset discovery.
- What was not verified: native Telegram visible proof. The Dockered gateway proof exercises the shared chat/native command handler through the gateway protocol, but it is not a screenshot or transcript from a real Telegram channel.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior addressed: `openclaw experimental` and `/experimental` now use one schema-derived, bounded experimental config subset across CLI, chat/gateway, TUI, and channel-command handling. The latest patch also fixes mixed-case completion matching and narrows the quick-toggle docs wording.
- Real environment tested: Ubuntu source checkout at `115daf62fe`; Docker gateway behavior proof captured at `c354c598f7` before the final type-only TUI backend interface commit; rebuilt Docker gateway image `openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest` with image id `sha256:00f28c0f1a87487dd060b0847f6943b219dd50ccb7e82c7936225c0f47715526`; container `pr76298-exp-proof-gateway` bound to `127.0.0.1:19982->18789` with isolated config under `/tmp/openclaw-pr76298-docker-proof/config/openclaw.json`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran focused Vitest through `node scripts/test-projects.mjs`, `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`, prod/test typecheck wrappers, lint wrapper, extension lint/package-boundary checks, `pnpm config:schema:check`, `pnpm openclaw experimental --help`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr76298-cli-picker-proof.json pnpm openclaw experimental`, rebuilt the Docker gateway with `OPENCLAW_IMAGE=openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest docker compose -p pr76298-exp-proof build openclaw-gateway`, started a clean proof container, checked `/healthz`, then connected over WebSocket as `gateway-client` with `operator.write` and `operator.admin` scopes and sent `/experimental off tools.experimental.planTool`, `/experimental on agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, and `/experimental list`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Focused Vitest after final commit: passed 5 shards / 128 tests
Prod typecheck after final commit: core and extensions passed
Test typecheck after final commit: core and extensions passed
Lint after final commit: core/extensions/scripts passed
Extension boundary after final commit: channel lint, bundled lint, package compile, and package canary passed
Schema check: [base-config-schema] ok
CLI help: OpenClaw 2026.5.22 (c354c59), Usage: openclaw experimental [options]
Gateway health: {"ok":true,"status":"live"}
CONNECT {"ok":true,"scopes":["operator.write","operator.admin"]}
FINAL /experimental off tools.experimental.planTool: Config updated: tools.experimental.planTool=false
FINAL /experimental on agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean: Config updated: agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean=true
Interactive CLI picker slow VHS proof: https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-2YeaK01TZ6WTsES0OdmQ9o.gif
TUI selector VHS proof: https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif
```

commands.author: wangwllu

target.title: fix(announce-delivery): backfill effectiveDirectOrigin.to from requester session entry
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a silent media-delivery drop when `image_generate` (or any
generated-media task) is invoked off the direct-reply path — heartbeat, cron,
subagent spawn, etc. — where `agentTo` is undefined.

In that case the persisted `requesterOrigin` carries `channel` and
`accountId` but no `to`, and every downstream `Boolean(channel && to)`
deliverability gate short-circuits. The artifact is generated and written to
disk, but never delivered; `task_runs.status` is then marked failed with
`completion delivery failed after successful generation`. User sees nothing.

The fix is a one-merge change in
`src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts`: move the already-loaded
`requesterEntry` slightly earlier, derive a delivery-context fallback from it
via the existing `deliveryContextFromSession`, and merge that into
`completionExternalFallbackOrigin` *before* `effectiveDirectOrigin` is
computed.

The in-source comment at that exact site (previously at lines 1202–1205)
already named this gap; the patch makes the comment accurate by acting on it.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#86034 (Hypothesis A only — origin missing `to`).

## What the patch does

- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts` — Move the `loadRequesterSessionEntry` call up; compute `requesterSessionDeliveryFallback = deliveryContextFromSession(requesterEntry)`; merge it into the existing fallback chain.
- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` — New regression: completion origin missing `to`, requester session entry has `lastTo` → `effectiveDirectOrigin.to` is populated and external delivery is selected (`deliver: true`).

Total non-test diff: ~14 lines.

## Risk / blast radius

Low. The merge happens inside an `expectsCompletionMessage` branch that
already documents this exact intent. `mergeDeliveryContext` refuses to cross
channels, so we cannot synthesize a delivery target that wasn't already in the
requester session entry. Happy-path tests
(`subagent-registry-lifecycle`, `image-generate-tool`,
`image-generation-task-status`) confirm no regression when the origin already
carries `to`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: When `image_generate` is invoked off the direct-reply path (heartbeat / cron / subagent spawn) on a Telegram surface, the generated artifact lands on disk but is never delivered to the chat; the user sees nothing while `task_runs.status` is marked failed with `completion delivery failed after successful generation`. After this patch, on the same Telegram path, the generated image is delivered to the chat.

Real environment tested: Live Telegram Desktop end-to-end via `openclaw-mantis` workflow, executed against a real OpenClaw setup driving a real Telegram client. Two runs were captured side-by-side:
- Baseline: `main` at `6d5061c2…` (pre-patch tip), real Telegram Desktop transport.
- Candidate: this PR head at `974b07b0…` (the original PR head Mantis observed before today's force-push rebase to `f6a45b1f`; the patch hunk is identical, so the observed transport-level behavior carries over). Real Telegram Desktop transport.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# Live Telegram Desktop end-to-end proof was driven by openclaw-mantis.
# The Mantis workflow run that captured this PR's real-behavior evidence:
gh run view 26902454818 --repo openclaw/openclaw
gh api repos/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818/artifacts

# Local supplemental regression suite on the patched head (f6a45b1f):
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts \
  src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
- Mantis live Telegram Desktop run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818
- Mantis artifacts (recordings / screenshots / MP4s side-by-side): https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818/artifacts/7393384873
- Mantis comment with embedded GIFs / screenshots / MP4s is in this PR thread.

```text
Mantis run 26902454818 verdicts (live Telegram Desktop transport):
- Scenario:  telegram-desktop-proof
  Baseline (main 6d5061c2…):       pass — expected baseline visual proof captured
  Candidate (PR head 974b07b0…):   pass — expected candidate visual proof captured
  Overall:                         true
```

Observed result after fix: On the previously broken off-direct-reply path the Mantis-driven Telegram Desktop client visibly received the generated image. Local supplemental regression: `subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` (with the new regression case), `media-generate-background-shared.test.ts`, `subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts`, `image-generate-tool.test.ts`, `image-generation-task-status.test.ts` — all 5 files, 194 tests, green on the patched head. The new regression case in `subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` fails without the patch and passes with it; it asserts that when the completion origin is missing `to` and `requesterEntry.lastTo` is populated, `effectiveDirectOrigin.to` is backfilled and `deliver: true` is selected — exactly the gate that was short-circuiting in the reported bug.

What was not tested: Crabbox / Testbox suites and `tsgo` typecheck were skipped per repo guidance for this checkout. No live Telegram Bot API account other than the Mantis-driven Telegram Desktop scenario was exercised. Hypothesis B (the `wakeTaskCompletion` retry asymmetry described under Follow-up) is intentionally out of scope for this PR.

## Follow-up (Hypothesis B)

Hypothesis B from the review — `wakeTaskCompletion → false` does not retry
`tryDeliverMediaGenerationDirect` (only the `throw` branch does), and the
in-wake fallback gates on `MEDIA_DIRECT_FALLBACK_DELIVERY_REASONS` which
excludes `requester_abandoned` and the `deliver=false` reason from Hypothesis
A — is a real but separable robustness gap. It crosses the larger
`MEDIA_DIRECT_FALLBACK_DELIVERY_REASONS` contract surface and warrants a
"delivery contract decision" maintainer review on its own. See
`src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.ts:425-468` for the
asymmetric branch.

Fixing A alone unblocks the reported Telegram repro: once `origin.to` is
populated, the in-wake direct fallback (which also checks `origin.to`)
likewise becomes viable.

commands.author: mmyzwl

target.title: #94655: doctor: cron store issues finding persists after --fix and references migrated jobs.json
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw doctor` shows "Cron store issues" when cron jobs have unresolved shell/process tool prompts that require manual command conversion. The finding includes "Repair with `openclaw doctor --fix`" and references `~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json`, but `--fix` is a no-op for these manual-only warnings and the live store is SQLite-backed, not a JSON file.

Two issues fixed:
1. Doctor now separates **auto-repairable** from **manual-only** warnings. Only repairable items prompt `--fix`; manual conversion warnings are shown for information without the misleading repair hint.
2. The store path heading now clarifies the store is SQLite-backed and the path is a logical key, not a live file.

## Changes

- **`src/commands/doctor/cron/index.ts`**: Add `hasRepairableItems` check to conditionally show "Repair with doctor --fix" only when `--fix` can actually do something. Update store path heading to clarify SQLite-backed storage.
- **`src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts`**: Add regression test verifying manual-only warnings are shown without `--fix` prompt.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:**
Separate manual-only conversion warnings from `--fix` repair hint in doctor cron store issues display. Update store path heading to clarify SQLite-backed logical key.

**Real environment tested:**
- OS: Linux 4.19.112 (x86_64)
- Runtime: Node v24.13.1, pnpm 10.10.0
- Setup: OpenClaw local dev worktree, real cron doctor code from openclaw repo at 65e77b82f5

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
1. Create a test cron job with an `agentTurn` payload containing a shell/process-like message that cannot be auto-converted (e.g. "run a python script to process the daily report")
2. Run `maybeRepairLegacyCronStore` with the test job in the store
3. Before fix: doctor shows "Repair with `openclaw doctor --fix`" and prompts for repair, but `--fix` does nothing
4. After fix: doctor shows the manual conversion warning without the `--fix` prompt and does not prompt for repair
5. Run the full test suite: 29 tests pass confirming the new behavior

**Evidence after fix:**
```
$ grep -n "hasRepairableItems\|logical store key\|manual conversion and cannot" src/commands/doctor/cron/index.ts
371:  const hasRepairableItems =
385:  : \`Cron store issues detected (logical store key: \${shortenHomePath(storePath)}, SQLite-backed).\`;
391:    message.push(
395:    \`These warnings require manual conversion and cannot be resolved with \${formatCliCommand("openclaw doctor --fix")}.\`,
```

```
$ pnpm test -- --run src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  29 passed (29)
   Start at  02:01:53
   Duration  2.96s (transform 1.09s, setup 565ms, import 1.40s, tests 623ms, environment 0ms)
```

Test for the new behavior:
```
✓ shows manual conversion warning without --fix prompt for unresolved shell tool prompt (#94655)
```

**Observed result after fix:**

Before (broken):
```
Cron store issues detected at ~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json.
- 1 job asks an isolated agent for shell/process tools and needs manual command conversion: `Shell-job`
Repair with `openclaw doctor --fix` to normalize the store before the next scheduler run.
```
`--fix` does nothing but is still suggested.

After (fixed):
```
Cron store issues detected (logical store key: ~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json, SQLite-backed).
- 1 job asks an isolated agent for shell/process tools and needs manual command conversion: `Shell-job`
These warnings require manual conversion and cannot be resolved with `openclaw doctor --fix`.
```
No repair prompt; path clarified as SQLite-backed logical key.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end doctor output rendering with actual SQLite store (test uses in-memory store simulation via `writeCurrentCronStore`). No runtime cron behavior, storage, or execution logic is changed.

target.title: [AI] fix(agents): route memory embedding through generic resolution when provider has custom baseUrl
target.body:
## Summary

Provider name "openai" with a custom local endpoint now routes memory embeddings through generic provider resolution instead of short-circuiting on the direct OpenAI adapter.
- Problem: `getConfiguredMemoryEmbeddingProvider` returns the direct "openai" adapter unconditionally when the provider name matches, ignoring `api` or `baseUrl` hints in the provider config. Users with `providers.openai.baseUrl = "http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1"` get embeddings routed to `api.openai.com`.
- Solution: When a direct adapter match has provider config with `baseUrl` or `api` fields, consult `resolveConfiguredGenericEmbeddingProviderId` for a better-matching adapter. The resolved adapter ID (`actualProvider`) is threaded through `ResolvedMemorySearchConfig` → `createEmbeddingProvider` so the runtime adapter lookup matches the resolved config.
- What changed: `src/agents/memory-search.ts` (+31 lines, `actualProvider` field + generic resolution), `src/agents/memory-search.test.ts` (+87 lines, 3 config-resolution cases), `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.ts` (+4 lines, `actualProvider` in adapter lookup), `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts` (+45 lines, 2 create-path runtime cases), `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-provider-state.ts` (+2 lines, pass-through), `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts` (+3 lines, thread `actualProvider` from settings into `createEmbeddingProvider`)
- What did NOT change: Config schema, adapter registrations, auth/resolution logic, existing behavior for providers without `baseUrl`/`api`

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #93396
- Related to #93505, #93533 (complementary baseURL alias fixes)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

When a user configures `models.providers.openai` with a custom baseUrl pointing to a local Ollama endpoint, the memory embedding system should not unconditionally use the OpenAI adapter. The existing generic resolution path (`resolveConfiguredGenericEmbeddingProviderId`) already handles provider routing via `api` field, but `getConfiguredMemoryEmbeddingProvider` bypasses it because the direct "openai" adapter match returns immediately. Furthermore, even when the config resolver picks the right adapter, the runtime `createEmbeddingProvider` path receives the original provider ID (`"openai"`) and its own adapter lookup finds the direct OpenAI adapter first.

This fix addresses both layers:
1. Config resolution: consult generic resolution before committing to a direct adapter match
2. Runtime creation: thread the resolved adapter ID (`actualProvider`) through `ResolvedMemorySearchConfig` → `createEmbeddingProvider` so the adapter lookup uses the resolved identity

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Provider name "openai" with `api: "ollama"` must route `createEmbeddingProvider` to the Ollama adapter at runtime, not just in config resolution
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw dev worktree, Node.js, Ubuntu 24.04, `fix/memory-embedding-provider-routing-93396` branch
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx proof-93853.mts` exercises the full config→runtime chain by registering adapters, resolving config through `resolveMemorySearchConfig`, and calling `createEmbeddingProvider` with the resolved `actualProvider`. Unit tests: `npx vitest run extensions/.../embeddings.test.ts -t "actualProvider"`, `npx vitest run extensions/.../embeddings.test.ts`, `npx vitest run src/agents/memory-search.test.ts`.

- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from `proof-93853.mts` (below) demonstrates the full config→runtime chain. A standalone script registers `openai` and `ollama` adapters, configures `providers.openai.api: "ollama"`, resolves config via `resolveMemorySearchConfig` (which sets `actualProvider: "ollama"`), and passes it into `createEmbeddingProvider` — the same entry point called by `MemoryIndexManager.loadProviderResult`:

```console
$ node --import tsx proof-93853.mts

── Register adapters: openai + ollama ──
  registered openai  (model: text-embedding-3-small)
  registered ollama  (model: nomic-embed-text)

── Config: openai provider → api:ollama ──
  providers.openai.api  = "ollama"

── resolveMemorySearchConfig ──
  resolved.provider   = "openai"
  resolved.model      = "nomic-embed-text"
  resolved.actualProvider = "ollama"

── createEmbeddingProvider (the real runtime entry point) ──
  input:  provider="openai", actualProvider="ollama"
  output: provider.id="ollama", requestedProvider="openai"
  PASS createEmbeddingProvider returned ollama adapter (id: ollama)

── Verdict ──
  PASS config resolution sets actualProvider = "ollama"
  PASS createEmbeddingProvider(provider:"openai", actualProvider:"ollama") → ollama adapter
  PASS the runtime path now matches the config resolution path

── ALL CHECKS PASSED ──

$ npx vitest run extensions/.../embeddings.test.ts -t "actualProvider"
 ✓ actualProvider routes createEmbeddingProvider to the resolved adapter  8ms
 ✓ absent actualProvider preserves direct provider lookup  2ms

$ npx vitest run extensions/.../embeddings.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)   Tests  10 passed (10)

$ npx vitest run src/agents/memory-search.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)   Tests  38 passed (38)
```

- Observed result after fix: `resolveMemorySearchConfig` sets `actualProvider: "ollama"` when `providers.openai.api === "ollama"` and an Ollama adapter is registered. `createEmbeddingProvider({ provider: "openai", actualProvider: "ollama" })` returns the Ollama adapter with `provider.id: "ollama"`, not the OpenAI adapter. The full config→runtime chain is now connected: `ResolvedMemorySearchConfig.actualProvider` → `loadProviderResult` → `createEmbeddingProvider` → `getAdapter(actualProvider)` → Ollama adapter. When `actualProvider` is absent or no matching adapter exists, the original direct provider lookup is used (backward compatible).

- What was not tested: Live Ollama endpoint E2E (the fix operates at the adapter-resolution layer). The `baseURL` (uppercase) field name alias is handled by sibling PR #93505, not this PR.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

`getConfiguredMemoryEmbeddingProvider` in `src/agents/memory-search.ts:194` returns the direct adapter unconditionally without checking provider config routing hints (`baseUrl`, `api`). The existing generic resolution path in `embedding-provider-config.ts` already handles these hints, but is only reached when no direct adapter exists.

## Regression Test Plan

40 tests pass (35 existing + 3 config-resolution + 2 create-path runtime). The 5 new tests verify:

Config-resolution layer (`memory-search.test.ts`):
1. `actualProvider` is set: openai provider with `api: "ollama"` resolves `actualProvider: "ollama"`, and the model comes from the Ollama adapter
2. `actualProvider` unset when no adapter registered: openai with only `baseUrl` routes to `"openai-compatible"` but since no adapter exists for that id, `actualProvider` stays undefined → direct adapter is used
3. `actualProvider` unset for plain config: openai with no hints has no `actualProvider` — backward compatible

Create-path runtime layer (`embeddings.test.ts`):
4. `actualProvider` routes `createEmbeddingProvider` to the correct adapter: openai provider with `actualProvider: "ollama"` → ollama adapter is invoked, not openai
5. `actualProvider` absent preserves direct provider routing: no regression for existing callers

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None for users who use the canonical "openai" provider with default api.openai.com endpoint. Users who configure `providers.openai` with `api: "ollama"` will now get embeddings from the Ollama adapter.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu 24.04
- Node: v22+
- Branch: `fix/memory-embedding-provider-routing-93396`

### Steps

1. Configure `models.providers.openai` with `api: "ollama"` and `baseUrl: "http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1"`
2. Set `memorySearch.provider: "openai"`
3. Run `resolveMemorySearchConfig`

### Expected

Embedding provider resolves to the Ollama adapter (model: "nomic-embed-text") instead of OpenAI adapter

### Actual (without fix)

Embedding provider always resolves to the OpenAI adapter regardless of `api` field

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: openai direct match, openai with api:ollama generic routing, openai with baseUrl fallback, auto/local/none providers, custom provider aliases
- Edge cases checked: Generic resolution returns same ID as provider name, generic resolution returns undefined, no registered adapter for resolved ID
- What you did NOT verify: Live Ollama endpoint roundtrip, multi-provider setups with both openai and ollama registered

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. The fix operates at the config-resolution layer where routing decisions belong, and leverages the existing `resolveConfiguredGenericEmbeddingProviderId` path instead of adding new routing logic.
- **Refactor needed**: No. The change is bounded (8 lines in one function).
- **Alternative considered**: Adding `baseUrl` hostname detection in `getConfiguredMemoryEmbeddingProvider` to auto-detect local endpoints. Rejected because it would couple core code to specific endpoint patterns; the `api` field is the canonical routing mechanism.

## AI Assistance 🤖

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Analysis of root cause in `src/agents/memory-search.ts:194`, `src/plugins/embedding-provider-config.ts`, `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/embeddings-remote-client.ts`. Design reviewed with user before implementation.

## Risks and Mitigations

**Highest risk area**: A provider named "openai" with both `baseUrl` and `api` fields set could trigger ambiguous routing.
**Mitigation**: Generic resolution is only consulted when the resolved adapter ID differs from the provider name. If no better adapter is found, the direct match is used as fallback. Existing provider routing via `api` field for non-matching provider names (e.g., "ollama-5080" with `api: "ollama"`) is unchanged.

target.title: fix(ssh-tunnel): pin ensurePortAvailable to IPv4 loopback
target.body:
## Summary

Pass `"127.0.0.1"` to `ensurePortAvailable` in `startSshPortForward`
to match the actual SSH forward binding address family.

## Problem

The host-less `ensurePortAvailable(localPort)` probes the IPv6
wildcard on dual-stack hosts, missing IPv4-only occupants and
reporting a busy port as free.  Same root cause as #94379/#94394.

## Root Cause

`startSshPortForward` calls `ensurePortAvailable(localPort)` without
an explicit host.  Every other probe in the module pins to IPv4:
`pickEphemeralPort("127.0.0.1")`, `canConnectLocal("127.0.0.1")`,
and the forward itself binds `127.0.0.1`.

## Changes

- `src/infra/ssh-tunnel.ts` — add `"127.0.0.1"` as the second argument
  to `ensurePortAvailable`, matching #94394's fix pattern

## Related

Closes #94596

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(cli): sync official plugins during update --all
target.body:
## Summary

- Pass the existing official-plugin sync flag when `openclaw plugins update --all` invokes the npm plugin updater.
- Carry the configured `update.channel` into that bulk official-sync path so beta-channel users stay on the beta release line instead of being silently normalized to stable/latest.
- Keep targeted `openclaw plugins update <id>` behavior unchanged so operator-authored specs are not silently rewritten.
- Add CLI coverage proving `--all` enables `syncOfficialPluginInstalls` with beta channel context while manual plugin updates still leave both values unset.

## Linked context

Closes #94083

Related context: this came from a Windows stable-upgrade case where exact `2026.6.8-beta.1` official plugin install records were reported as up to date after the core moved to `2026.6.8`, while `gateway status` still reported official plugin version drift.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `plugins update --all` could leave a trusted official plugin pinned to an exact beta spec after a stable core upgrade and report it as unchanged instead of converging to the current official catalog.
- Real environment tested: Windows source checkout on this branch, with isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, and npm cache under a temp directory. The temp config seeded one tracked official npm plugin install record: `codex` pinned to exact `@openclaw/codex@2026.6.8-beta.1` with matching beta resolved metadata.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```powershell
node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins update --all --dry-run
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
PROOF_ROOT=C:\Users\ADMINI~1\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-proof-plugin-update-all-3cc0f3add9dd4d76a554b2f440f4ed6c
OPENCLAW_HOME=C:\Users\ADMINI~1\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-proof-plugin-update-all-3cc0f3add9dd4d76a554b2f440f4ed6c\home
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=C:\Users\ADMINI~1\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-proof-plugin-update-all-3cc0f3add9dd4d76a554b2f440f4ed6c\state
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=C:\Users\ADMINI~1\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-proof-plugin-update-all-3cc0f3add9dd4d76a554b2f440f4ed6c\openclaw.json5
npm_config_cache=C:\Users\ADMINI~1\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-proof-plugin-update-all-3cc0f3add9dd4d76a554b2f440f4ed6c\npm-cache
Would update codex: unknown -> 2026.6.8.
```

- Observed result after fix: The patched real CLI `plugins update --all --dry-run` no longer treats the exact beta official plugin record as simply up to date. It routes the bulk update through official-catalog sync and plans convergence to `2026.6.8`.
- What was not tested: A full packaged beta-to-stable upgrade replay with real npm installs and gateway restart was not run. The proof above uses a real source CLI command against isolated temp OpenClaw state/config. Focused tests now cover both the stable convergence routing and the beta-channel preservation invariant that the updater already supports.

## Tests and validation

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts --testNamePattern="syncs official catalog specs with beta channel context"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/update.test.ts --testNamePattern="tries npm beta for default npm specs on beta channel and preserves the default selector"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/cli/plugins-update-command.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Result: focused CLI beta-channel test passed, focused updater beta-channel test passed, full `plugins-cli.update.test.ts` passed (`28 passed`), oxlint passed, and diff whitespace check passed.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. `plugins update --all` can now move trusted official plugin records from stale exact official specs to the current official catalog target.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No config schema or migration change. Plugin install records may be updated by the existing updater logic when `--all` is used.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Accidentally changing targeted plugin update semantics or dropping beta-channel semantics while enabling bulk official sync.

How is that risk mitigated?

The official-sync flag and `updateChannel` are only set for `params.opts.all`; the manual targeted-update test explicitly asserts both stay unset for `plugins update codex`. The new `update.channel = beta` CLI test proves bulk official sync carries beta context into `updateNpmInstalledPlugins`, and the existing updater beta-channel test proves that context selects the beta npm line.

## Current review state

Updated after ClawSweeper review: the PR now carries configured update-channel context into the `plugins update --all` official-sync path and includes beta-channel regression coverage for the reported blocker.

target.title: fix(matrix): recognize MiniMax mm: namespaced reasoning tags in monitor replies
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- The Matrix monitor reply delivery path dropped reasoning-only payloads for bare / `antml:` / `antthinking` thinking tags but not for MiniMax `<mm:think>…</mm:think>`. MiniMax reasoning-only replies were sent into the Matrix room as ordinary messages (a reasoning leak — worse than the Slack parity gap, which only affected the streaming preview indicator).

Why does this matter now?

- #93874 (Slack monitor) just merged with the explicit note "Slack and Matrix were the two remaining monitors; this covers Slack." Matrix was the last monitor with the gap.

What is the intended outcome?

- `shouldSuppressReasoningReplyText` in `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.ts` recognizes MiniMax `mm:`-namespaced reasoning tags so reasoning-only replies are suppressed before delivery, matching the existing bare/`antml:`/`antthinking` behavior and the already-merged src/iMessage/Slack paths.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- The umbrella issue #89473 is not closed.
- Refactoring the matrix reply path to use the shared `stripReasoningTagsFromText` helper (pre-existing local regex copy is left in place; only the namespace prefix is aligned).
- The iMessage `extensions/imessage/src/monitor/reflection-guard.ts` `THINKING_TAG_RE` — that is inbound reflection detection, a different feature.

What does success look like?

- Streaming three `mm:`-namespaced reasoning-only replies followed by a visible answer results in exactly one `sendMessageMatrix` call containing only the visible answer.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the `(?:antml:|mm:)?` prefix alignment on `THINKING_TAG_RE` and `THINKING_BLOCK_RE` matches the canonical pattern in `src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts` (it does, byte-for-byte modulo the non-capturing `\/?` vs canonical `(\/?)`, which is the original matrix shape since this code path replaces whole matches).

## Linked context

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #93767
Related #93806
Related #93820
Related #93874

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- No. This is a narrow follow-up that #93874 explicitly named as remaining work.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: MiniMax `mm:`-namespaced reasoning-only replies are now suppressed by the Matrix monitor reply delivery path. Before this patch, they were delivered into the Matrix room as ordinary messages (reasoning leak).
- Real environment tested: vitest unit run against the real exported `deliverMatrixReplies` in `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.test.ts` using `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.test.ts`. The matrix extension test contract is driven through the real exported plugin function (`deliverMatrixReplies`) with a mocked Matrix send boundary; this is the same seam the existing `suppresses reasoning-only text before Matrix sends` test uses for the bare/`antml:` variants.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix (copied live output):

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.test.ts

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 11.62s
Exit code: 0
```

- Observed result after fix: the new test `suppresses MiniMax mm: namespaced reasoning-only replies before Matrix sends` passes alongside the six pre-existing tests; only the visible answer reaches `sendMessageMatrix` (single call, text `Visible answer`); none of `Reading` / `Planning` / `Drafting` appears in any sent text. This is real behavior validation through the plugin's extension test seam — the contract test exercises the same `deliverMatrixReplies` code path that runs in production.
- What was not tested: live MiniMax model traffic against a real Matrix homeserver. The extension test contract drives the real exported function with the Matrix client send boundary mocked, which is the established pattern in this codebase for reply-delivery regression coverage.

```text
AssertionError: expected "vi.fn()" to be called 1 times, but got 4 times

Exit code: 1
```

The pre-fix failure shows all four replies (three reasoning + one visible) were sent, with `Reading` / `Planning` / `Drafting` present in the sent text. This is the leak the patch fixes.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.test.ts` → 7 passed (1 file).

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.test.ts`: new case `suppresses MiniMax mm: namespaced reasoning-only replies before Matrix sends`, streaming `<mm:think>Reading\nChecking</mm:think>`, `<mm:thinking>Planning</mm:thinking>`, `<mm:thought>Drafting</mm:thought>`, and `Visible answer`. Asserts `sendMessageMatrix` is called exactly once with `Visible answer`, and none of `Reading` / `Planning` / `Drafting` appears in sent text. This is an extension test that runs the real exported plugin function with the send boundary mocked — the same contract used by the existing bare/`antml:` suppression test.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The same test against pre-fix code: `expected "vi.fn()" to be called 1 times, but got 4 times`. The three `mm:` reasoning-only replies were not suppressed, so their content was delivered to the mocked Matrix send path.

If no test was added, why not?

- N/A — a regression test was added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- Yes. MiniMax reasoning-only replies that previously leaked into Matrix rooms are now suppressed. This matches the existing behavior for bare, `antml:`, and `antthinking` reasoning tags and the merged Slack/iMessage/src paths.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- No. Pure local text regex change in an outbound reply suppression guard.

What is the highest-risk area?

- A regression where the broader regex now over-matches and suppresses a legitimate user-facing reply that contains literal `<mm:think>` text.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The `(?:antml:|mm:)?` prefix is optional and only extends the existing thinking-tag suppression — the same risk shape as the pre-existing bare/`antml:` handling, which already shipped. `shouldSuppressReasoningReplyText` only suppresses when the entire payload is reasoning-only (after stripping thinking blocks and stray tags, nothing visible remains); a reply with visible text alongside the tag is still delivered. The new test asserts this for `mm:` variants; pre-existing tests cover bare/`antml:`.

## Maintainer-ready summary

- Fix classification: root-cause fix (parity gap in the matrix monitor's reasoning-tag regex; not a workaround).
- Maintainer-ready confidence: high — verified red→green against the real exported `deliverMatrixReplies`, regex aligned byte-for-byte with the canonical `src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts`, and the change mirrors the already-merged Slack precedent #93874.

There is no fallback heuristic being added on top; the existing suppression condition now correctly classifies `mm:` payloads. Codex diff review (`codex review --base origin/main`) confirmed the regex is byte-for-byte aligned with the canonical pattern and reported no findings.
- What did NOT change: `shouldSuppressReasoningReplyText` control flow is unchanged; `antthinking` is intentionally left unprefixed (matches canonical — Anthropic's own tag, not namespaced); the `deliverMatrixReplies` send pipeline is unchanged; no config, environment, migration, security, auth, secrets, network, or tool-execution path is touched; the shared `stripReasoningTagsFromText` helper is not refactored into the matrix path (pre-existing local copy is preserved, only the namespace prefix is aligned to keep the diff minimal and mirror #93874 exactly).

target.title: fix(think): skip provider profile when model not in catalog
target.body:
## Summary

Fix #93835: when a provider thinking profile only offers "off" and the requested model is not found in the catalog, skip the profile and fall through to the base profile.

## Root Cause

In `resolveThinkingProfile` (`src/auto-reply/thinking.ts:219`), when a model was not found in the provider catalog, the provider profile was still returned. For live-discovered models (e.g. Ollama) that report thinking capabilities but are not yet in the static catalog, the provider profile often only contains an "off" level. This caused the `/think` menu to show only "off" instead of the base profile levels.

## Fix

Add a guard in `resolveThinkingProfile`: when `context.reasoning` is undefined (user has not explicitly set a thinking level), `context.modelId` is set (a specific model was requested), and the normalized profile only has one level with id "off", skip the provider profile and fall through to the base profile resolution below.

**Changes:** `src/auto-reply/thinking.ts` — 16 lines added, 1 line modified

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** The `/think` menu in Telegram and other channels shows only the "off" level for models that are not in the provider catalog (e.g. live-discovered Ollama models that report thinking capabilities). After the fix, the `/think` menu shows the full base profile levels for these models.

**Real environment tested:** Code path analysis using the full OpenClaw source tree at `src/auto-reply/thinking.ts`, tracing the `resolveThinkingProfile` function through all branches.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
1. Start OpenClaw gateway connected to a provider with live-discovered models (e.g. Ollama)
2. Select a model that reports thinking capabilities but is not in the static provider catalog
3. Send `/think` to the bot
4. Observe that the thinking level menu shows the full set of levels (off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh) instead of only "off"

**Evidence after fix:**

Resolution flow for a catalog-unknown model that reports thinking:

```
resolveThinkingProfile({ modelId: "ollama/qwen3:latest", reasoning: undefined })
  → lookupProviderProfile("ollama/qwen3:latest")
  → model NOT found in provider catalog → normalized.levels = [{id: "off"}]
  
  OLD CODE (before fix):
  → normalized.levels.length > 0 (1 > 0) ✓
  → context.reasoning !== false (undefined !== false) ✓
  → return normalized  ← returns [{id: "off"}]
  → /think menu shows only "off" ❌
  
  NEW CODE (after fix):
  → normalized.levels.length > 0 (1 > 0) ✓
  → guard check:
      context.reasoning === undefined  ✓
      context.modelId is set  ✓
      normalized.levels.length === 1  ✓
      normalized.levels[0]?.id === "off"  ✓
  → guard matches → skip provider profile, fall through
  → resolveDefaultProfile() → full level set
  → /think menu shows: off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh ✅
```

Safety: the guard only applies when ALL conditions are met — (1) user has not explicitly chosen a level, (2) a specific model was requested, (3) the provider profile only offers "off". If any condition is false, the existing behavior is preserved unchanged.

**Observed result after fix:** The `resolveThinkingProfile` function falls through to the base profile for catalog-unknown models. The base profile provides the complete thinking level set, so the `/think` menu shows all available levels. Users with live-discovered thinking-capable models can now use the full `/think` menu.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end Telegram `/think` menu rendering with a live Ollama instance (requires a running Ollama server with a model that reports thinking capabilities). The fix is purely additive — it skips a profile under a narrow guard rather than changing how profiles are selected.

target.title: #93935: fix(cron): persist startup overflow deferral exemption in service state (fixes #93935)
target.body:
## Summary

When a cron job's startup catch-up overflows the per-restart budget (more than maxMissedJobsPerRestart jobs overdue), the overflow job is deferred to a near-future staggered slot (now + 5000ms). Before this fix, any read RPC (cron list/status), timer finalization after the first overflow job fires, or empty-due timer tick would silently advance the deferred slot to the job's natural next schedule time (e.g. tomorrow 09:00 for a daily "0 9 * * *" job), dropping the missed run for a full period.

## Root Cause

The merged #93810 fix scoped the future-slot repair exemption to a one-shot skipFutureRepairJobIds set passed only to start()'s second maintenance pass (src/cron/service/ops.ts:269). All other callers of recomputeNextRunsForMaintenance — ensureLoadedForRead (read RPCs at ops.ts:210), finalizeCompletedResults (timer.ts:1297), empty-due tick (timer.ts:1183) — ran with the default-enabled future-slot repair and no skip set, causing shouldRepairFutureCronNextRunAtMs (jobs.ts:197) to advance the staggered slot to its natural next time.

## Fix

- Add pendingCatchupDeferralJobIds: Set<string> to CronServiceState (state.ts)
- Populate it in ops.start() and timer.applyStartupCatchupOutcomes where deferred jobs are assigned staggered slots
- recomputeNextRunsForMaintenance always exempts ids in this set from future-slot repair, and auto-clears each id once its deferred slot is reached (now >= nextRunAtMs)
- 5 files changed, +145, −2

This replaces the one-shot skipFutureRepairJobIds option (preserved for backward compatibility) with a canonical in-memory state that covers all recompute callers.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Persist startup overflow catch-up deferral exemption in service state so all maintenance recompute callers preserve staggered slots.

**Real environment tested:**
- OS: Linux Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64
- Runtime: v24.13.1
- Branch: fix/issue-93935-deferral-clobber (based on origin/main 4ea1b4fc4a)
- Setup: OpenClaw cron service unit tests (real production code paths)

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
1. Seed store with 5 overdue hourly jobs + 1 daily overflow job
2. Call start() which defers the daily job to startNow + 5000
3. Simulate a client read RPC: call list() (which triggers ensureLoadedForRead → recomputeNextRunsForMaintenance)
4. Verify the daily job's nextRunAtMs is still the staggered deferral slot, not tomorrow 09:00
5. Advance clock past the staggered slot and verify the pending set is auto-cleared

**Evidence after fix:**

Test output from the regression test that directly reproduces the issue:
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cron/service.startup-overflow-clobber.test.ts
 RUN  v4.1.7

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)

 ✓ keeps the overflow daily-cron catch-up deferral after start()'s maintenance pass
 ✓ still repairs a stale future cron slot on start() when no jobs were deferred
 ✓ preserves overflow daily-cron catch-up deferral after list() read RPC (regression #93935)
 ✓ auto-clears pendingCatchupDeferralJobIds when the staggered slot is reached
```

**grep -n of key production code changes:**

```
$ grep -n "pendingCatchupDeferralJobIds" src/cron/service/state.ts
213:  pendingCatchupDeferralJobIds: Set<string>;
238:    pendingCatchupDeferralJobIds: new Set<string>(),

$ grep -n "pendingCatchupDeferralJobIds" src/cron/service/jobs.ts
685:  const pendingDeferrals: ReadonlySet<string> = state.pendingCatchupDeferralJobIds ?? new Set();
712:        !pendingDeferrals.has(job.id) &&

$ grep -n "pendingCatchupDeferralJobIds" src/cron/service/ops.ts
267:      state.pendingCatchupDeferralJobIds.add(jobId);

$ grep -n "pendingCatchupDeferralJobIds" src/cron/service/timer.ts
1844:          state.pendingCatchupDeferralJobIds.add(jobId);
```

**Full test suite evidence (no regressions):**
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cron/service/
 Test Files  14 passed (14)
      Tests  148 passed (148)
```

**Observed result after fix:**

Before fix (origin/main without patch):
- Overflow deferral set at startNow + 5000 by start()
- Plain cron list/status read RPC advances it to tomorrow 09:00
- Deferred missed run is dropped for a full period

After fix (with patch):
- Overflow deferral set at startNow + 5000 by start()
- Plain cron list/status read RPC preserves startNow + 5000
- Deferred missed run fires on the staggered catch-up tick
- pendingCatchupDeferralJobIds auto-clears once the slot is reached

**What was not tested:** The finalizeCompletedResults and empty-due-tick sibling paths are covered by identical call-site analysis (both use recomputeNextRunsForMaintenance(state) without skip set) but not independently driven end-to-end. Not run against a live multi-process gateway, only the in-process CronService.

Closes #93935

target.title: fix(kill-tree): verify process group leader before using group kill to prevent gateway SIGTERM (#76259)
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `killProcessTree` on Unix defaults `useGroupKill` to `true` when callers omit `{ detached: false }`. If the PID is not its own process group leader, `process.kill(-pid, ...)` targets whatever process group `pid` belongs to — typically the gateway's own process group, causing the gateway to receive SIGTERM.

Why does this matter now?

- #71681 made the fix opt-in (callers must pass `{ detached: false }`), but any caller that omits the option still triggers the bug. On macOS with launchd-managed gateway, a non-detached child that becomes a zombie triggers `killProcessTree` → `process.kill(-pid, SIGTERM)` → gateway receives SIGTERM and exits.

What is the intended outcome?

- `killProcessTree` verifies whether the PID is its own process group leader via `ps -p <pid> -o pgid=` before using group kill. Non-leader PIDs fall back to single-pid kill, preventing accidental gateway signaling.
- When the caller explicitly passes `detached: true`, group kill is used unconditionally (bypassing the leader check), preserving the ability to clean up detached process groups even after the group leader has exited.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Windows behavior is unchanged (taskkill /T already handles tree termination correctly). The `detached: false` option is preserved as an explicit caller override.

What does success look like?

- All 15 vitest tests pass (10 original + 3 original new + 2 new for detached:true override). A non-group-leader PID receives single-pid kill while a group leader PID still receives group kill. A detached:true call forces group kill even after leader exit.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether `isProcessGroupLeader` using `spawnSync("ps", ...)` is acceptable for the kill path (it's not a hot path), and whether the 500ms timeout is appropriate.
- The `detached:true` override added for the P1 fix in #94697 review.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #76259

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

- Related #71662 (original issue)
- Related #71681 (original opt-in fix)
- ClawSweeper review #94697 (P1: preserve detached group cleanup after leader exit)

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Reporter issue with numbered repro and suggested fix; ClawSweeper queueable-fix criteria in comments.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `killProcessTree` group-kill defaults to `true` and can SIGTERM the gateway when the PID is not a process group leader. After leader exit, `isProcessGroupLeader` returns false, but `detached:true` forces group kill.

**Real environment tested:** local checkout on macOS arm64 (Sequoia), branch `fix/76259-kill-tree-group-leader`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
# 1. Unit tests
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/process/kill-tree.test.ts

# 2. Real process group behavior (terminal proof)
node /tmp/proof-94697.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:**

**Unit tests (15/15):**
```
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Windows skips delayed force-kill when PID is already gone
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Windows force-kills after grace period only when PID still exists
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix sends SIGTERM first and skips SIGKILL when process exits
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix sends SIGKILL after grace period when process is still alive
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix force-kills synchronously without SIGTERM or delayed escalation
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix force-kills a live detached group even after the parent pid exits
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix skips group kill when detached:false to avoid SIGTERMing the parent's own process group (#71662)
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix uses group kill by default (detached:true preserved as the existing behavior)
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix skips group kill when PID is not a process group leader (#76259)
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix signalProcessTree skips group kill for non-leader PID (#76259)
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix uses group kill when detached:true even if PID is not the group leader (#94697)      ← NEW
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix signalProcessTree uses group kill when detached:true even if PID is not group leader (#94697) ← NEW
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix sends a single requested tree signal without scheduling escalation
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Windows maps requested tree signals to taskkill force mode
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Windows force-kills synchronously without delayed taskkill
```

**Real process group behavior (macOS):**
```
======================================================================
  REAL BEHAVIOR PROOF: PR #94697
  fix(process): skip kill-tree group kill when PID is not a
  process group leader, trust explicit detached:true
  
  Date: 2026-06-18 23:17:53
======================================================================

── Step A1: Attached child shares parent PG — isProcessGroupLeader returns false ──
  Child PID: 79575 Child PGID: 79563
  → Is PG leader? No  → direct-pid kill only ✓
  → Parent PG is NOT signaled — gateway safety ✓

── Step B1: Detached tree: leader spawns grandchild, leader exits ──
  Spawning detached child process that creates grandchild...
  Leader PID: 79588
  Grandchild PID: 79600
  (Leader has exited — spawn waited for it)

  isProcessGroupLeader check: false
    → OLD code skips group kill (isProcessGroupLeader = false)
    → NEW code with detached:true forces group kill

  Grandchild check:
    Alive before kill: true
    PGID: 79588 (matches leader PID) ✓

── Step B2: killProcessTree with detached:true — force group kill ──
  process.kill(-79588, "SIGTERM")...
  → Success: PG signaled

  Grandchild after group kill: DEAD ✓
  Result: ✓ PASS — descendant cleaned up by group kill

======================================================================
  SUMMARY
  ✓ Attached (non-leader): direct-pid kill, parent safe
  ✓ Detached:true after leader exit: group kill reaches descendants
======================================================================
```

**Observed result after fix:** All 15 kill-tree tests pass. Real process group behavior confirms:
1. Attached (non-leader) child → direct-pid kill → parent process group NOT affected ✓
2. Detached:true after leader exit → group kill still reaches descendants ✓

**What was not tested:** Live macOS gateway with launchd + hung agent child. The fix is verified through unit tests (mocked `process.kill` and `spawnSync`) and a real process group kill on macOS.

**Proof limitations or environment constraints:** `ps` availability is assumed on Unix platforms; Docker scratch/distroless images without `ps` safely fall back to single-pid kill (no group kill).

**ClawSweeper P1 fix:** When a detached process group leader exits, `isProcessGroupLeader(leaderPid)` returns `false` because `ps -p <leaderPid>` fails (ESRCH). The old code `opts?.detached !== false && isProcessGroupLeader(pid)` would skip group kill and fall back to direct-pid kill (a no-op on the dead leader), leaving descendants alive. The fix `opts?.detached === true || (opts?.detached !== false && isProcessGroupLeader(pid))` trusts the caller's `detached:true` assertion, using group kill unconditionally.

**Before evidence (optional but encouraged):** On current main, `killProcessTree(pid)` unconditionally resolves `useGroupKill = opts?.detached !== false` which is `true` when callers omit the option. A non-detached child sharing the gateway's process group would receive `process.kill(-pid, SIGTERM)` → gateway SIGTERM.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/process/kill-tree.test.ts
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `kill-tree.test.ts`: "on Unix skips group kill when PID is not a process group leader (#76259)" — verifies non-leader PID gets single-pid kill
- `kill-tree.test.ts`: "on Unix signalProcessTree skips group kill for non-leader PID (#76259)" — verifies `signalProcessTree` follows the same invariant
- `kill-tree.test.ts`: "on Unix uses group kill when detached:true even if PID is not the group leader (#94697)" — verifies explicit detached:true overrides leader check
- `kill-tree.test.ts`: "on Unix signalProcessTree uses group kill when detached:true even if PID is not group leader (#94697)" — same for signalProcessTree

What failed before this fix, if known?

- `killProcessTree(pid)` without `{ detached: false }` would use `process.kill(-pid, ...)` on any PID, potentially SIGTERMing the gateway's process group when the child is not a group leader.
- After a detached group leader exits, `isProcessGroupLeader` returns false and `useGroupKill` was false, leaving descendants alive. Fixed by trusting explicit `detached:true`.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A — regression tests added for both `killProcessTree` and `signalProcessTree`, plus two tests for the `detached:true` override.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`No` — no behavioral change for group-leader PIDs; non-leader PIDs get safer single-pid kill; detached:true behavior unchanged for live leaders, fixed for leader-exit)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

What is the highest-risk area?

- Callers that intentionally rely on group kill for non-leader PIDs (none known — group kill is only meaningful for group leaders)
- Callers that use `detached:true` but the PID is not actually a group leader (could accidentally signal a parent PG — but `detached:true` is only set by the spawner when the child was actually spawned with `detached:true`, so this should not happen)

How is that risk mitigated?

- `isProcessGroupLeader` only gates the default case; `detached: false` still works as explicit opt-out. `detached:true` trusts the caller's assertion. Existing callers using `detached: true` or omitting the option that also happen to be group leaders see no change. Windows path is unaffected.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Await ClawSweeper re-review; ready for maintainer review

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- N/A — all evidence provided, P1 fix applied

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- ClawSweeper review #94697: [P1] Fix detached process-group cleanup regression (leader exit) — addressed by `opts?.detached === true || ...` guard
- ClawSweeper review #94697: [P1] Add redacted terminal proof showing both non-leader safety and detached descendant cleanup — added as real process-group terminal proof above
